CITE EVIDENCE:’’ It has to belong to somebody,’’ Mrs. Price  keeps saying, but nobody can remember . It’s an ugly  sweater with red plastic buttons and a collar and sleeves all stretched out like you could use it for a jump rope. It’s maybe a thousand years old and even if it belonged to me I wouldn’t say so.  EXPLAIN: Rachel uses a smilie  to compare the sweater to a jump rope . Rachel makes this comparison to show it’s not hers. CITE EVIDENCE: “  I'm eleven and it's my birthday today, and I'm crying like I'm three in front of everybody.”. EXPLAIN:  Rachel uses this simile to compare her actions to a three-year-old. She is using this comparison to show that she is upset.
